acceptance.assertion: A versioned Python call-reference artifact records deterministic call sites, callee expressions, source ranges, enclosing caller symbols and explicit resolution states, bound to the canonical dump digest.
Only uniquely resolvable local definitions receive target symbol ids; attribute, alias, dynamic and ambiguous calls remain visibly unresolved or candidate-only.
MCP exposes bounded find_references and get_callers tools with exact-first matching, stable ordering, input validation, explicit truncation and live freshness on every response.
Existing find_symbol, symbol-index v1, bundle consumers and profiles remain compatible; missing call-graph artifacts fail closed without hidden refresh.
Schema, producer, consumer, MCP stdio, ambiguity, recursion, method-call, parse-error and live-bundle tests pass together with the complete Lenskit suite and GitHub CI.
A fixed navigation goldset demonstrates recovery of expected callers/references with no regression to find_symbol; default promotion remains separate.
The result makes no complete call graph, runtime reachability, dynamic dispatch, dependency completeness, test sufficiency, review or merge-readiness claim.

approval.note: Use only the isolated branch/worktree. Preserve static, read-only, freshness-bound and non-authoritative semantics.

goal: Produce a deterministic Python static call-reference index and expose bounded read-only reference/caller lookups through RepoBrief MCP with exact source ranges, freshness and explicit ambiguity.

metadata.superseded_planning_reference: heimgewebe/lenskit#1005 was the earlier find_symbol precursor and did not implement the call graph.

validation.github_ci: PASS on the exact heads of RepoGround PRs #1018 and #1022; all substantive repository, security and contract checks passed. The optional Claude check on #1022 was skipped.
validation.fresh_targeted_command: python3 -m pytest -q merger/repoground/tests/test_python_call_graph.py merger/repoground/tests/test_call_navigation.py merger/repoground/tests/test_mcp_stdio_e2e.py merger/repoground/tests/test_bundle_manifest_integration.py -k 'call_graph or find_references or get_callers or tools_list'
validation.implementation_ancestry: PASS; merge commits 48d1797553ddb233a9b64b22e50436ca5c6ac291 and f22bc5b119f0e61b6fa637d11d2c4e6959798637 are ancestors of the fresh main head.

verified_acceptance.rscg-v1-t001-artifact: PASS; PR #1018 adds the versioned python-call-graph artifact, schema, producer, manifest binding, exact source ranges, caller symbols and explicit resolution states.
verified_acceptance.rscg-v1-t001-resolution: PASS; tests and proof constrain target ids to uniquely resolved local definitions while ambiguous, dynamic, attribute and unresolved calls remain explicit rather than invented.
verified_acceptance.rscg-v1-t001-tools: PASS; find_references and get_callers are exposed through MCP with validated bounded inputs, exact-first matching, stable ordering, truncation metadata and live bundle freshness checks.
verified_acceptance.rscg-v1-t001-compatibility: PASS; find_symbol and existing bundle/profile consumers remain covered, while missing, stale, malformed or hash-mismatched call-graph evidence fails closed without hidden refresh.
verified_acceptance.rscg-v1-t001-validation: PASS; PR #1018 and #1022 CI passed on their exact heads, and the fresh main-head rerun passed 37 focused producer, navigation, MCP and bundle tests.
verified_acceptance.rscg-v1-t001-utility: PASS; the committed fixed acceptance tier exercises 20 reference, 20 caller and 20 target queries at 50,000 calls, with byte-equivalent linear/indexed/MCP answers and preserved find_symbol coverage.
verified_acceptance.rscg-v1-t001-nonclaims: PASS; proofs and task metadata explicitly deny complete call-graph, runtime reachability, dynamic dispatch, dependency completeness, test sufficiency, review completeness, merge readiness and default-promotion authority.

metadata.outcome: RepoGround main contains a deterministic evidence-bound Python call graph plus bounded find_references and get_callers MCP navigation. The later in-memory index preserves byte-equivalent public results while avoiding repeated linear scans. Static ambiguity and all non-authoritative boundaries remain explicit.
metadata.self_review: PASS: closeout is bound to two merged implementation commits, their exact heads and CI, the fresh RepoGround main head, committed proofs/measurement, and a fresh receipt-backed 37-test rerun. It does not infer runtime reachability, dynamic dispatch, complete dependency coverage, general performance, test sufficiency or default promotion.

title: Add bounded find_references and get_callers navigation
